verdict_reasoning: B2M is a 11.8 kDa protein with no TM domain of its own, but reaches the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane as a stable non-covalent partner of MHC class I heavy chains. It is co-trafficked intracellularly with MHC α chains, forms a noncovalent complex at the surface, and is essential for stabilizing both the structure and surface expression of MHC class I molecules. B2M associates with MHC class I, CD1, MR1, FcRn, and other class I-like molecules, and is displayed on nearly all nucleated cells.
